title: Part 3 – Collaborative Task (C1 Cambridge)
description: Evaluate the management of the interaction: have you invited your partner to speak or have you monopolized the conversation?
prompt: Acts as an official examiner for the Cambridge C1 Advanced (CAE). Evaluate my intervention in Part 3 (Collaborative Task) according to the official Cambridge criteria: Grammar and Vocabulary, Discourse Management, Pronunciation and Interactive Communication. Rate each criterion from 1 to 5. Check if I have invited my partner to participate (correct) or if I have monopolized the conversation (incorrect). Identify the fillers I have used and suggest alternatives C1. Next, give me 5 C1 level expressions that fit what I said. Here is my intervention: [PASTE YOUR TEXT]

criteria.name: Grammar and Vocabulary
criteria.description: Use of negotiation vocabulary, agreement and disagreement, and sophisticated opinion structures.

criteria.name: Discourse Management
criteria.description: Ability to organize and develop collaborative discussion, contributing ideas and responding to those of the partner.

criteria.name: Pronunciation
criteria.description: Clarity and naturalness when interacting in real time with your partner.

criteria.name: Interactive Communication
criteria.description: Key skill: invite your partner to participate, respond to their ideas and manage speaking turns.

tips: Use expressions to rephrase: 'In other words...', 'What I mean to say is...', 'To put it another way...'
Muestra acuerdo matizado: 'I see where you're coming from, though I'd argue that...', 'That's a valid point, and to build on that...'
Muestra desacuerdo educado: 'I'm not entirely convinced that...', 'I'd take a slightly different view on that...'
Gestiona el tiempo: 'Perhaps we should consider...', 'We might want to move on to...', 'Shall we try to reach a decision?'
Use expressions to rephrase: 'In other words...', 'What I mean to say is...', 'To put it another way...'

speakingFeatures.focus: Collaborative discussion and negotiation with a partner
speakingFeatures.examinerRole: Sets up the task and monitors; may intervene to keep the discussion on track
speakingFeatures.commonMistake: Monopolizing the conversation without inviting the partner to contribute
